judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with the same numbers and recommendation; comparable, with Report 2 adding slightly more repo-grounded detail (overwrite/no-resume caveats).

report_1.evidence: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 avg tokens per scoring call ≈ 9,360,000 tokens (~9.4M)... northpeak (primary/default): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10,000,000-token window... Launching run_nightly.sh as-is would exhaust northpeak's remaining 640k tokens partway through

report_2.evidence: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 average tokens per scoring call = ≈ 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M)... Northpeak (primary, default): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10,000,000-token window. The run needs ~14.6× what's left... would exhaust the allowance roughly 7% of the way through

report_1.evidence: Instead I'd route the whole run to the secondary backend... --provider secondary... ridgeline has 10.8M tokens free versus a 9.36M need

report_2.evidence: Route to the secondary backend by invoking score_batch.py directly with --provider secondary... Ridgeline's 10.8M remaining tokens comfortably cover the run

report_1.evidence: I would not run the default path. Launching run_nightly.sh as-is would exhaust northpeak's remaining 640k tokens partway through... Instead I'd route the whole run to the secondary backend

report_2.evidence: I would not run run_nightly.sh as-is, because it silently uses the primary/northpeak backend, which has only ~640k tokens left tonight... --limit only takes the first N transcripts... a clean single run on secondary is the practical choice

report_1.evidence: the usage snapshot is ~3 hours stale (19:25Z vs. 22:30Z now), so before launching I'd refresh the telemetry (or at minimum confirm ridgeline's remaining balance) rather than trust an old number

report_2.evidence: The snapshot is from 19:25Z, ~3 hours stale at the assumed 22:30Z launch; I'd re-capture it (or check the vendor dashboards) to confirm ridgeline still has ≥ ~9.4M tokens before committing
